article_title: Name and territory of the Union
summary: Defines India, that is Bharat, as a Union of States and identifies the territory of India.

article_title: Admission or establishment of new States
summary: Empowers Parliament to admit or establish new States on terms it thinks fit.

article_title: Sikkim to be associated with the Union
summary: Omitted article linked to Sikkim’s earlier associate status before becoming a State.

article_title: Formation of new States and alteration of areas, boundaries or names of existing States
summary: Gives Parliament power to form new States and alter boundaries, areas or names of existing States.

article_title: Laws made under articles 2 and 3 to provide for amendment of First and Fourth Schedules and supplemental matters
summary: Clarifies that laws under Articles 2 and 3 may amend Schedules without being treated as constitutional amendments under Article 368.

article_title: Citizenship at the commencement of the Constitution
summary: Defines who became citizens of India at the commencement of the Constitution.

article_title: Rights of citizenship of certain persons who migrated to India from Pakistan
summary: Deals with citizenship rights of persons who migrated from Pakistan to India.

article_title: Rights of citizenship of certain migrants to Pakistan
summary: Covers citizenship consequences for persons who migrated to Pakistan and later returned to India.

article_title: Rights of citizenship of certain persons of Indian origin residing outside India
summary: Provides for citizenship of persons of Indian origin residing outside India.

article_title: Persons voluntarily acquiring citizenship of a foreign State not to be citizens
summary: States that voluntary acquisition of foreign citizenship results in loss of Indian citizenship.

article_title: Continuance of the rights of citizenship
summary: Protects continuance of citizenship subject to laws made by Parliament.

article_title: Parliament to regulate the right of citizenship by law
summary: Empowers Parliament to make laws regarding acquisition and termination of citizenship.

article_title: Definition
summary: Defines State for the purpose of Fundamental Rights.

article_title: Laws inconsistent with or in derogation of the fundamental rights
summary: Provides that laws inconsistent with Fundamental Rights are void to the extent of inconsistency.

article_title: Equality before law
summary: Guarantees equality before law and equal protection of laws.

article_title: Prohibition of discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex or place of birth
summary: Prohibits discrimination and enables special provisions for women, children, socially backward classes and EWS.

article_title: Equality of opportunity in matters of public employment
summary: Guarantees equality in public employment and allows reservation-related provisions.

article_title: Abolition of Untouchability
summary: Abolishes untouchability and forbids its practice in any form.

article_title: Abolition of titles
summary: Prohibits titles except military and academic distinctions.

article_title: Protection of certain rights regarding freedom of speech, etc.
summary: Protects six freedoms including speech, assembly, association, movement, residence and profession, subject to reasonable restrictions.

article_title: Protection in respect of conviction for offences
summary: Protects against ex post facto laws, double jeopardy and self-incrimination.

article_title: Protection of life and personal liberty
summary: Protects life and personal liberty except according to procedure established by law.

article_title: Right to education
summary: Makes free and compulsory education a Fundamental Right for children aged 6 to 14 years.

article_title: Protection against arrest and detention in certain cases
summary: Provides safeguards against arrest and lays down rules for preventive detention.

article_title: Prohibition of traffic in human beings and forced labour
summary: Prohibits human trafficking, begar and forced labour.

article_title: Prohibition of employment of children in factories, etc.
summary: Prohibits employment of children below 14 years in factories, mines and hazardous occupations.

article_title: Freedom of conscience and free profession, practice and propagation of religion
summary: Protects religious freedom subject to public order, morality, health and other constitutional provisions.

article_title: Freedom to manage religious affairs
summary: Gives religious denominations the right to manage religious affairs and property.

article_title: Freedom as to payment of taxes for promotion of any particular religion
summary: Prevents compulsory taxation for promotion or maintenance of any particular religion.

article_title: Freedom as to attendance at religious instruction or worship in certain educational institutions
summary: Regulates religious instruction in educational institutions based on State funding and management.

article_title: Protection of interests of minorities
summary: Protects the right of citizens to conserve distinct language, script or culture.

article_title: Right of minorities to establish and administer educational institutions
summary: Allows religious and linguistic minorities to establish and administer educational institutions.

article_title: Compulsory acquisition of property
summary: Omitted by the 44th Amendment; right to property is now a constitutional legal right under Article 300A.

article_title: Saving of laws providing for acquisition of estates, etc.
summary: Protects certain land reform and related laws from challenge under Articles 14 and 19.

article_title: Validation of certain Acts and Regulations
summary: Protects laws placed in the Ninth Schedule, subject to basic structure review after I.R. Coelho.

article_title: Saving of laws giving effect to certain directive principles
summary: Protects laws implementing Articles 39(b) and 39(c) from challenge under Articles 14 and 19.

article_title: Remedies for enforcement of rights conferred by this Part
summary: Gives the right to move the Supreme Court for enforcement of Fundamental Rights.

article_title: Power of Parliament to modify rights in their application to Forces, etc.
summary: Allows Parliament to restrict Fundamental Rights of armed forces, police and intelligence-related services.

article_title: Restriction on rights while martial law is in force in any area
summary: Allows Parliament to indemnify acts done during martial law.

article_title: Legislation to give effect to the provisions of this Part
summary: Gives Parliament exclusive power to make laws for certain Fundamental Rights provisions.

article_title: Definition
summary: Defines State for the purpose of Directive Principles.

article_title: Application of the principles contained in this Part
summary: States that DPSPs are not enforceable by courts but are fundamental in governance.

article_title: State to secure a social order for the promotion of welfare of the people
summary: Directs the State to promote welfare and reduce inequalities.

article_title: Certain principles of policy to be followed by the State
summary: Contains key socio-economic principles including livelihood, resource distribution and prevention of concentration of wealth.

article_title: Equal justice and free legal aid
summary: Directs the State to provide equal justice and free legal aid.

article_title: Organisation of village panchayats
summary: Directs the State to organise village panchayats as units of self-government.

article_title: Right to work, to education and to public assistance in certain cases
summary: Directs the State to provide work, education and public assistance within economic capacity.

article_title: Provision for just and humane conditions of work and maternity relief
summary: Directs the State to ensure humane work conditions and maternity relief.

article_title: Living wage, etc., for workers
summary: Directs the State to secure living wage and decent standard of life for workers.

article_title: Participation of workers in management of industries
summary: Directs the State to secure worker participation in management of industries.

article_title: Promotion of co-operative societies
summary: Directs the State to promote voluntary, democratic and autonomous co-operative societies.

article_title: Uniform civil code for the citizens
summary: Directs the State to endeavour to secure a uniform civil code throughout India.

article_title: Provision for early childhood care and education to children below the age of six years
summary: Directs the State to provide early childhood care and education for children below six years.

article_title: Promotion of educational and economic interests of Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes and other weaker sections
summary: Directs the State to promote interests of SCs, STs and weaker sections and protect them from exploitation.

article_title: Duty of the State to raise nutrition, standard of living and public health
summary: Directs the State to improve nutrition, living standards and public health, including prohibition-related goals.

article_title: Organisation of agriculture and animal husbandry
summary: Directs the State to organise agriculture and animal husbandry on modern and scientific lines.

article_title: Protection and improvement of environment and safeguarding of forests and wildlife
summary: Directs the State to protect the environment, forests and wildlife.

article_title: Protection of monuments and places and objects of national importance
summary: Directs the State to protect monuments, places and objects of national importance.

article_title: Separation of judiciary from executive
summary: Directs the State to separate the judiciary from the executive in public services.

article_title: Promotion of international peace and security
summary: Directs the State to promote international peace, treaty respect and arbitration.

article_title: Fundamental duties
summary: Lists Fundamental Duties of citizens including constitutional respect, harmony, environment protection and scientific temper.

article_title: The President of India
summary: Provides that there shall be a President of India.

article_title: Executive power of the Union
summary: Vests Union executive power in the President, to be exercised according to the Constitution.

article_title: Election of President
summary: Provides the electoral college for election of the President.

article_title: Manner of election of President
summary: Lays down proportional representation and vote value principles for presidential election.

article_title: Term of office of President
summary: Fixes the President’s term at five years, subject to resignation, removal or continuation until successor assumes office.

article_title: Eligibility for re-election
summary: Makes the President eligible for re-election.

article_title: Qualifications for election as President
summary: Specifies citizenship, age, Lok Sabha qualification and office-of-profit restrictions for President.

article_title: Conditions of President’s office
summary: Specifies conditions including no membership of legislature and no other office of profit.

article_title: Oath or affirmation by the President
summary: Provides the President’s oath to preserve, protect and defend the Constitution.

article_title: Procedure for impeachment of the President
summary: Lays down impeachment procedure for violation of the Constitution.

article_title: Time of holding election to fill vacancy in the office of President and term of office of person elected to fill casual vacancy
summary: Provides time limits for presidential elections and vacancy filling.

article_title: The Vice-President of India
summary: Provides that there shall be a Vice-President of India.

article_title: The Vice-President to be ex officio Chairman of the Council of States
summary: Makes the Vice-President the ex officio Chairman of Rajya Sabha.

article_title: Vice-President to act as President or discharge functions during vacancy or absence
summary: Provides for the Vice-President acting as President during vacancy, absence or inability.

article_title: Election of Vice-President
summary: Provides the electoral college and procedure for election of the Vice-President.

article_title: Term of office of Vice-President
summary: Fixes the Vice-President’s term and removal procedure.

article_title: Time of holding election to fill vacancy in the office of Vice-President and term of office of person elected to fill casual vacancy
summary: Provides timing rules for Vice-Presidential elections and vacancies.

article_title: Oath or affirmation by the Vice-President
summary: Provides the Vice-President’s oath or affirmation.

article_title: Discharge of President’s functions in other contingencies
summary: Allows Parliament to provide for discharge of President’s functions in other contingencies.

article_title: Matters relating to election of President or Vice-President
summary: Gives the Supreme Court jurisdiction over disputes relating to presidential and vice-presidential elections.

article_title: Power of President to grant pardons, etc.
summary: Gives the President power to grant pardon, reprieve, respite, remission, suspension or commutation in specified cases.

article_title: Extent of executive power of the Union
summary: Defines the extent of Union executive power with reference to Parliament’s legislative competence and treaty obligations.

article_title: Council of Ministers to aid and advise President
summary: Provides for a Council of Ministers headed by the Prime Minister to aid and advise the President.

article_title: Other provisions as to Ministers
summary: Deals with appointment, tenure, collective responsibility, oath and salaries of Union Ministers.

article_title: Attorney-General for India
summary: Provides for appointment, qualifications, duties and rights of the Attorney-General for India.

article_title: Conduct of business of the Government of India
summary: Provides rules for executive action and business allocation of the Union Government.

article_title: Duties of Prime Minister as respects furnishing information to President, etc.
summary: Specifies duties of the Prime Minister in communication with the President.

article_title: Constitution of Parliament
summary: Provides that Parliament consists of the President, Rajya Sabha and Lok Sabha.

article_title: Composition of the Council of States
summary: Specifies the composition of Rajya Sabha, including elected and nominated members.

article_title: Composition of the House of the People
summary: Specifies the composition and territorial representation principles of Lok Sabha.

article_title: Readjustment after each census
summary: Provides for delimitation and readjustment of constituencies after each census.

article_title: Duration of Houses of Parliament
summary: Provides that Rajya Sabha is permanent and Lok Sabha normally lasts five years.

article_title: Qualification for membership of Parliament
summary: Specifies qualifications for becoming a member of Parliament.

article_title: Sessions of Parliament, prorogation and dissolution
summary: Gives the President power to summon, prorogue and dissolve Houses, with six-month session gap rule.

article_title: Right of President to address and send messages to Houses
summary: Gives the President the right to address and send messages to Parliament.

article_title: Special address by the President
summary: Provides for President’s special address at the first session after general election and first session each year.

article_title: Rights of Ministers and Attorney-General as respects Houses
summary: Allows Ministers and Attorney-General to speak and participate in Parliament without voting right if not members.

article_title: The Chairman and Deputy Chairman of the Council of States
summary: Provides for Rajya Sabha Chairman and Deputy Chairman.

article_title: Vacation and resignation of, and removal from, the office of Deputy Chairman
summary: Provides rules for vacancy, resignation and removal of Rajya Sabha Deputy Chairman.

article_title: Power of Deputy Chairman or other person to perform duties of Chairman
summary: Provides who performs Rajya Sabha Chairman’s duties during vacancy or absence.

article_title: Chairman or Deputy Chairman not to preside while resolution for removal is under consideration
summary: Bars Chairman or Deputy Chairman from presiding during consideration of removal resolution against them.

article_title: The Speaker and Deputy Speaker of the House of the People
summary: Provides for election of Lok Sabha Speaker and Deputy Speaker.

article_title: Vacation and resignation of, and removal from, offices of Speaker and Deputy Speaker
summary: Provides rules for vacancy, resignation and removal of Lok Sabha Speaker and Deputy Speaker.

article_title: Power of Deputy Speaker or other person to perform duties of Speaker
summary: Provides who performs Speaker’s duties during vacancy or absence.

article_title: Speaker or Deputy Speaker not to preside while resolution for removal is under consideration
summary: Bars Speaker or Deputy Speaker from presiding during consideration of removal resolution against them.

article_title: Salaries and allowances of Chairman, Deputy Chairman, Speaker and Deputy Speaker
summary: Provides for salaries and allowances of presiding officers of Parliament.

article_title: Secretariat of Parliament
summary: Provides for separate secretarial staff for each House of Parliament.

article_title: Oath or affirmation by members
summary: Requires MPs to make oath or affirmation before taking their seats.

article_title: Voting in Houses, power of Houses to act notwithstanding vacancies and quorum
summary: Deals with voting, quorum and validity of parliamentary proceedings despite vacancies.

article_title: Vacation of seats
summary: Deals with situations where a Member of Parliament’s seat becomes vacant, including dual membership, resignation and disqualification.

article_title: Disqualifications for membership
summary: Lists grounds on which a person is disqualified from being an MP, including office of profit, unsound mind, insolvency, citizenship and law-made disqualifications.

article_title: Decision on questions as to disqualifications of members
summary: Says the President decides questions of MP disqualification after obtaining the Election Commission’s opinion.

article_title: Penalty for sitting and voting before making oath or when not qualified or disqualified
summary: Imposes penalty on a person who sits or votes in Parliament without proper qualification, oath or despite disqualification.

article_title: Powers, privileges, etc., of the Houses of Parliament and of the members and committees thereof
summary: Protects freedom of speech in Parliament and parliamentary privileges of members and committees.

article_title: Salaries and allowances of members
summary: Provides that MPs receive salaries and allowances as decided by Parliament by law.

article_title: Provisions as to introduction and passing of Bills
summary: Explains general rules for introduction and passage of ordinary Bills in Parliament.

article_title: Joint sitting of both Houses in certain cases
summary: Allows a joint sitting of Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha when there is a deadlock over an ordinary Bill.

article_title: Special procedure in respect of Money Bills
summary: Gives Lok Sabha dominance over Money Bills and limits Rajya Sabha to recommendations within 14 days.

article_title: Definition of Money Bills
summary: Defines what counts as a Money Bill and makes the Speaker’s certification central.

article_title: Assent to Bills
summary: Explains the President’s options after a Bill is passed: assent, withholding assent or return, except for Money Bills.

article_title: Annual financial statement
summary: Provides for the Union Budget, called the annual financial statement, showing receipts and expenditure.

article_title: Procedure in Parliament with respect to estimates
summary: Deals with parliamentary voting on demands for grants and separates charged expenditure from voted expenditure.

article_title: Appropriation Bills
summary: Provides for Appropriation Bills that legally authorise withdrawal of money from the Consolidated Fund of India.

article_title: Supplementary, additional or excess grants
summary: Allows extra parliamentary approval when authorised funds are insufficient or excess spending has occurred.

article_title: Votes on account, votes of credit and exceptional grants
summary: Provides temporary or special grants when normal budget approval is pending or unusual expenditure arises.

article_title: Special provisions as to financial Bills
summary: Deals with Financial Bills and President’s recommendation for certain financial proposals.

article_title: Rules of procedure
summary: Allows each House of Parliament to make rules for its procedure and conduct of business.

article_title: Regulation by law of procedure in Parliament in relation to financial business
summary: Allows Parliament to regulate procedure for financial business by law.

article_title: Language to be used in Parliament
summary: Allows business in Parliament in Hindi or English, with limited permission for members to use their mother tongue.

article_title: Restriction on discussion in Parliament
summary: Bars discussion of the conduct of Supreme Court or High Court judges except during removal proceedings.

article_title: Courts not to inquire into proceedings of Parliament
summary: Protects parliamentary proceedings from being questioned in court for procedural irregularities.

article_title: Power of President to promulgate Ordinances during recess of Parliament
summary: Gives the President ordinance-making power when Parliament is not in session and urgent action is needed.

article_title: Establishment and constitution of Supreme Court
summary: Establishes the Supreme Court of India and deals with appointment, qualifications, age and removal of judges.

article_title: National Judicial Appointments Commission
summary: Inserted to create the NJAC for judicial appointments, but the amendment was struck down by the Supreme Court in 2015.

article_title: Functions of Commission
summary: Listed the proposed functions of the NJAC, but it became ineffective after the NJAC judgment.

article_title: Power of Parliament to make law
summary: Allowed Parliament to regulate NJAC-related procedure, but it became ineffective after the NJAC judgment.

article_title: Salaries, etc., of Judges
summary: Deals with salaries, privileges, allowances and pension of Supreme Court judges.

article_title: Appointment of acting Chief Justice
summary: Allows appointment of an acting Chief Justice of India when the office is vacant or the CJI is unable to perform duties.

article_title: Appointment of ad hoc Judges
summary: Allows ad hoc judges to sit in the Supreme Court when quorum of permanent judges is lacking.

article_title: Attendance of retired Judges at sittings of the Supreme Court
summary: Allows retired Supreme Court or High Court judges to sit and act as Supreme Court judges with consent.

article_title: Supreme Court to be a court of record
summary: Makes the Supreme Court a court of record with power to punish for contempt.

article_title: Seat of Supreme Court
summary: States that the Supreme Court sits in Delhi or another place fixed by the Chief Justice with presidential approval.

article_title: Original jurisdiction of the Supreme Court
summary: Gives the Supreme Court exclusive original jurisdiction in certain disputes involving the Union and States.

article_title: Exclusive jurisdiction of the Supreme Court in regard to questions as to constitutional validity of Central laws
summary: Omitted article that had given exclusive jurisdiction to the Supreme Court over constitutional validity of Central laws.

article_title: Appellate jurisdiction of Supreme Court in appeals from High Courts in certain cases
summary: Provides appeals to the Supreme Court from High Court judgments involving substantial constitutional questions.

article_title: Appellate jurisdiction of Supreme Court in appeals from High Courts in regard to civil matters
summary: Deals with civil appeals from High Courts to the Supreme Court.

article_title: Appellate jurisdiction of Supreme Court in regard to criminal matters
summary: Deals with criminal appeals from High Courts to the Supreme Court.

article_title: Certificate for appeal to the Supreme Court
summary: Provides procedure for High Courts to grant certificates for appeal to the Supreme Court.

article_title: Jurisdiction and powers of the Federal Court under existing law to be exercisable by the Supreme Court
summary: Transfers certain pre-Constitution Federal Court jurisdiction to the Supreme Court.

article_title: Special leave to appeal by the Supreme Court
summary: Gives the Supreme Court wide discretionary power to hear appeals through special leave.

article_title: Review of judgments or orders by the Supreme Court
summary: Allows the Supreme Court to review its own judgments or orders.

article_title: Enlargement of the jurisdiction of the Supreme Court
summary: Allows Parliament to expand the jurisdiction and powers of the Supreme Court.

article_title: Conferment on the Supreme Court of powers to issue certain writs
summary: Allows Parliament to give the Supreme Court power to issue writs for purposes beyond Fundamental Rights.

article_title: Transfer of certain cases
summary: Allows the Supreme Court to transfer or withdraw cases involving similar substantial legal questions.

article_title: Ancillary powers of Supreme Court
summary: Allows Parliament to give the Supreme Court extra powers needed for effective functioning.

article_title: Law declared by Supreme Court to be binding on all courts
summary: Makes Supreme Court-declared law binding on all courts in India.

article_title: Enforcement of decrees and orders of Supreme Court and orders as to discovery, etc.
summary: Allows the Supreme Court to pass orders necessary for complete justice in any case before it.

article_title: Power of President to consult Supreme Court
summary: Allows the President to seek the Supreme Court’s advisory opinion on important legal or factual questions.

article_title: Civil and judicial authorities to act in aid of the Supreme Court
summary: Requires all civil and judicial authorities in India to assist the Supreme Court.

article_title: Special provisions as to disposal of questions relating to constitutional validity of laws
summary: Omitted article that had created special bench and majority requirements for constitutional validity decisions.

article_title: Rules of Court, etc.
summary: Allows the Supreme Court to frame rules for its practice and procedure with presidential approval.

article_title: Officers and servants and the expenses of the Supreme Court
summary: Deals with appointment, service conditions and expenses of Supreme Court staff.

article_title: Interpretation
summary: Defines expressions used in the chapter on the Supreme Court.

article_title: Comptroller and Auditor-General of India
summary: Creates the office of CAG and protects its independence through appointment, removal and service safeguards.

article_title: Duties and powers of the Comptroller and Auditor-General
summary: Says the CAG performs duties and exercises powers prescribed by Parliament.

article_title: Form of accounts of the Union and of the States
summary: Provides that Union and State accounts are kept in the form prescribed by the President on CAG’s advice.

article_title: Audit reports
summary: Requires CAG reports to be submitted to the President or Governor and then laid before the legislature.

article_title: Definition
summary: Defines State for Part VI and excludes Jammu and Kashmir under the original constitutional framework.

article_title: Governors of States
summary: Provides that each State has a Governor, and the same person may be Governor of multiple States.

article_title: Executive power of State
summary: Vests the executive power of the State in the Governor, to be exercised according to the Constitution.

article_title: Appointment of Governor
summary: Provides that the Governor is appointed by the President.

article_title: Term of office of Governor
summary: States that the Governor holds office during the President’s pleasure and normally has a five-year term.

article_title: Qualifications for appointment as Governor
summary: Requires the Governor to be an Indian citizen and at least 35 years old.

article_title: Conditions of Governor’s office
summary: Sets conditions for the Governor’s office, including no legislative membership and no office of profit.

article_title: Oath or affirmation by the Governor
summary: Provides the Governor’s oath to preserve, protect and defend the Constitution.

article_title: Discharge of the functions of the Governor in certain contingencies
summary: Allows the President to make arrangements for discharge of Governor’s functions in contingencies.

article_title: Power of Governor to grant pardons, etc.
summary: Gives the Governor power to grant pardon, reprieve, respite, remission, suspension or commutation in State-law offences.

article_title: Extent of executive power of State
summary: Defines the State executive power with reference to matters on which the State Legislature can make laws.

article_title: Council of Ministers to aid and advise Governor
summary: Provides for a Council of Ministers headed by the Chief Minister to aid and advise the Governor, except where discretion applies.

article_title: Other provisions as to Ministers
summary: Deals with appointment, tenure, collective responsibility, oath and salaries of State Ministers.

article_title: Special provision as to certain Ministers in the State of Mizoram
summary: Omitted special provision related to ministerial arrangements in Mizoram.

article_title: Advocate-General for the State
summary: Provides for appointment, qualifications, duties and rights of the Advocate-General of a State.

article_title: Conduct of business of the Government of a State
summary: Provides rules for executive action and business allocation of the State Government.

article_title: Duties of Chief Minister as respects furnishing information to Governor, etc.
summary: Specifies the Chief Minister’s duties in communicating information and decisions to the Governor.

article_title: Constitution of Legislatures in States
summary: Provides for State Legislatures and identifies States with bicameral legislatures.

article_title: Abolition or creation of Legislative Councils in States
summary: Allows Parliament to create or abolish a State Legislative Council after a special resolution by the State Assembly.

article_title: Composition of the Legislative Assemblies
summary: Deals with the composition, territorial constituencies and seat distribution of State Legislative Assemblies.

article_title: Composition of the Legislative Councils
summary: Specifies the composition of State Legislative Councils, including elected and nominated members.

article_title: Duration of State Legislatures
summary: Provides normal duration of State Legislative Assemblies and continuing nature of Legislative Councils.

article_title: Qualification for membership of the State Legislature
summary: Specifies qualifications for membership of State Legislatures.

article_title: Sessions of the State Legislature, prorogation and dissolution
summary: Gives the Governor power to summon, prorogue and dissolve the State Legislature, with six-month session gap rule.

article_title: Right of Governor to address and send messages to the House or Houses
summary: Gives the Governor the right to address and send messages to the State Legislature.

article_title: Special address by the Governor
summary: Provides for Governor’s special address at the first session after election and first session each year.

article_title: Rights of Ministers and Advocate-General as respects the Houses
summary: Allows Ministers and the Advocate-General to speak and participate in State Legislature proceedings without voting right if not members.

article_title: The Speaker and Deputy Speaker of the Legislative Assembly
summary: Provides for election of Speaker and Deputy Speaker of the State Legislative Assembly.

article_title: Vacation and resignation of, and removal from, the offices of Speaker and Deputy Speaker
summary: Provides rules for vacancy, resignation and removal of Assembly Speaker and Deputy Speaker.

article_title: Power of the Deputy Speaker or other person to perform the duties of the office of, or to act as, Speaker
summary: Provides who performs Speaker’s duties during vacancy or absence in a State Assembly.

article_title: Speaker or Deputy Speaker not to preside while resolution for removal is under consideration
summary: Bars Speaker or Deputy Speaker from presiding during consideration of removal resolution against them.

article_title: The Chairman and Deputy Chairman of the Legislative Council
summary: Provides for Chairman and Deputy Chairman of a State Legislative Council.

article_title: Vacation and resignation of, and removal from, the offices of Chairman and Deputy Chairman
summary: Provides rules for vacancy, resignation and removal of Legislative Council Chairman and Deputy Chairman.

article_title: Power of the Deputy Chairman or other person to perform duties of Chairman
summary: Provides who performs Chairman’s duties during vacancy or absence in a Legislative Council.

article_title: Chairman or Deputy Chairman not to preside while resolution for removal is under consideration
summary: Bars Chairman or Deputy Chairman from presiding during consideration of removal resolution against them.

article_title: Salaries and allowances of Speaker, Deputy Speaker, Chairman and Deputy Chairman
summary: Provides for salaries and allowances of State Legislature presiding officers.

article_title: Secretariat of State Legislature
summary: Provides for separate secretarial staff for State Legislatures.

article_title: Oath or affirmation by members
summary: Requires State legislators to make oath or affirmation before taking their seats.

article_title: Voting in Houses, power of Houses to act notwithstanding vacancies and quorum
summary: Deals with voting, quorum and validity of State Legislature proceedings despite vacancies.

article_title: Vacation of seats
summary: Deals with situations where a State legislator’s seat becomes vacant, including dual membership, resignation and disqualification.

article_title: Disqualifications for membership
summary: Lists grounds on which a person is disqualified from being a member of a State Legislature.

article_title: Decision on questions as to disqualifications of members
summary: Says the Governor decides questions of State legislator disqualification after obtaining the Election Commission’s opinion.

article_title: Penalty for sitting and voting before making oath or when not qualified or disqualified
summary: Imposes penalty on a person who sits or votes in a State Legislature without proper qualification, oath or despite disqualification.

article_title: Powers, privileges, etc., of the Houses of Legislatures and of the members and committees thereof
summary: Protects freedom of speech in State Legislatures and legislative privileges of members and committees.

article_title: Salaries and allowances of members
summary: Provides that State legislators receive salaries and allowances as decided by the State Legislature by law.

article_title: Provisions as to introduction and passing of Bills
summary: Explains general rules for introduction and passage of ordinary Bills in State Legislatures.

article_title: Restriction on powers of Legislative Council as to Bills other than Money Bills
summary: Limits the power of Legislative Councils to delay ordinary Bills passed by Legislative Assemblies.

article_title: Special procedure in respect of Money Bills
summary: Gives the State Legislative Assembly dominance over Money Bills and limits the Legislative Council to recommendations.

article_title: Definition of Money Bills
summary: Defines Money Bills for State Legislatures and makes the Speaker’s certification central.

article_title: Assent to Bills
summary: Explains the Governor’s options after a Bill is passed, including assent, withholding assent, return or reservation for the President.

article_title: Bills reserved for consideration
summary: Deals with the President’s decision on Bills reserved by the Governor.

article_title: Annual financial statement
summary: Provides for the State Budget, called the annual financial statement.

article_title: Procedure in Legislature with respect to estimates
summary: Explains voting on demands for grants in State Legislatures.

article_title: Appropriation Bills
summary: Allows money to be withdrawn from the State Consolidated Fund after legislative approval.

article_title: Supplementary, additional or excess grants
summary: Allows extra approval when State funds are insufficient or excess expenditure occurs.

article_title: Votes on account, votes of credit and exceptional grants
summary: Allows temporary or exceptional State grants when normal budget approval is pending.

article_title: Special provisions as to financial Bills
summary: Deals with introduction and recommendation requirements for State financial Bills.

article_title: Rules of procedure
summary: Allows each State House to make rules for its procedure.

article_title: Regulation by law of procedure in the Legislature of the State in relation to financial business
summary: Allows State law to regulate procedure for financial business.

article_title: Language to be used in the Legislature
summary: Allows State legislative business in official language, Hindi or English.

article_title: Restriction on discussion in the Legislature
summary: Bars discussion of conduct of Supreme Court or High Court judges except during removal process.

article_title: Courts not to inquire into proceedings of the Legislature
summary: Protects State legislative proceedings from court challenge for procedural irregularity.

article_title: Power of Governor to promulgate Ordinances during recess of Legislature
summary: Gives Governors ordinance-making power when the Legislature is not in session.

article_title: High Courts for States
summary: Provides that every State shall have a High Court.

article_title: High Courts to be courts of record
summary: Makes High Courts courts of record with power to punish contempt.

article_title: Constitution of High Courts
summary: Deals with composition of High Courts.

article_title: Appointment and conditions of the office of a Judge of a High Court
summary: Deals with appointment, tenure, age and qualifications of High Court judges.

article_title: Application of certain provisions relating to Supreme Court to High Courts
summary: Applies some Supreme Court judge provisions to High Court judges.

article_title: Oath or affirmation by Judges of High Courts
summary: Provides oath for High Court judges.

article_title: Restriction on practice after being a permanent Judge
summary: Restricts post-retirement practice by permanent High Court judges.

article_title: Salaries, etc., of Judges
summary: Deals with salaries, allowances and pension of High Court judges.

article_title: Transfer of a Judge from one High Court to another
summary: Allows transfer of High Court judges by the President after consultation.

article_title: Appointment of acting Chief Justice
summary: Allows appointment of an acting Chief Justice of a High Court.

article_title: Appointment of additional and acting Judges
summary: Allows appointment of additional and acting High Court judges.

article_title: Appointment of retired Judges at sittings of High Courts
summary: Allows retired judges to sit in High Courts with consent.

article_title: Jurisdiction of existing High Courts
summary: Preserves jurisdiction and powers of existing High Courts subject to law.

article_title: Power of High Courts to issue certain writs
summary: Gives High Courts broad writ jurisdiction for rights and other legal purposes.

article_title: Constitutional validity of Central laws not to be considered in proceedings under article 226
summary: Omitted article that restricted High Courts from considering validity of Central laws.

article_title: Power of superintendence over all courts by the High Court
summary: Gives High Courts supervisory power over subordinate courts and tribunals.

article_title: Transfer of certain cases to High Court
summary: Allows High Courts to withdraw cases involving substantial constitutional questions.

article_title: Special provisions as to disposal of questions relating to constitutional validity of State laws
summary: Omitted article that imposed special rules for deciding validity of State laws.

article_title: Officers and servants and the expenses of High Courts
summary: Deals with High Court staff and administrative expenses.

article_title: Extension of jurisdiction of High Courts to Union territories
summary: Allows Parliament to extend High Court jurisdiction to Union territories.

article_title: Establishment of a common High Court for two or more States
summary: Allows a common High Court for multiple States or Union territories.

article_title: Repealed
summary: Repealed provision related to earlier constitutional arrangements.

article_title: Appointment of district judges
summary: Deals with appointment, posting and promotion of district judges.

article_title: Validation of appointments of, and judgments, etc., delivered by, certain district judges
summary: Validates certain district judge appointments and actions.

article_title: Recruitment of persons other than district judges to the judicial service
summary: Deals with recruitment to State judicial service below district judge level.

article_title: Control over subordinate courts
summary: Gives High Courts control over district courts and subordinate judiciary.

article_title: Interpretation
summary: Defines terms used for subordinate courts.

article_title: Application of the provisions of this Chapter to certain class or classes of magistrates
summary: Allows application of subordinate judiciary provisions to certain magistrates.

article_title: Repealed
part_title: The States in Part B of the First Schedule
summary: Repealed after abolition of Part B States by the Seventh Amendment.

article_title: Administration of Union territories
summary: Provides that Union territories are administered by the President through an Administrator.

article_title: Creation of local Legislatures or Council of Ministers or both for certain Union territories
summary: Allows local legislatures or ministers for certain Union territories.

article_title: Special provisions with respect to Delhi
summary: Creates special constitutional arrangement for the National Capital Territory of Delhi.

article_title: Provision in case of failure of constitutional machinery
summary: Allows special action when Delhi’s constitutional machinery fails.

article_title: Power of administrator to promulgate Ordinances during recess of Legislature
summary: Gives ordinance power to administrators of certain Union territories.

article_title: Power of President to make regulations for certain Union territories
summary: Allows the President to make regulations for specified Union territories.

article_title: High Courts for Union territories
summary: Deals with High Courts for Union territories.

article_title: Repealed
summary: Repealed provision related to earlier Union territory administration.

article_title: Definitions
summary: Defines key terms used in the Panchayats Part.

article_title: Gram Sabha
summary: Recognises Gram Sabha and allows State law to define its powers.

article_title: Constitution of Panchayats
summary: Requires Panchayats at village, intermediate and district levels.

article_title: Composition of Panchayats
summary: Deals with composition and election of Panchayat members.

article_title: Reservation of seats
summary: Provides reservation in Panchayats for SCs, STs and women.

article_title: Duration of Panchayats, etc.
summary: Fixes Panchayat term at five years and provides election rules after dissolution.

article_title: Disqualifications for membership
summary: Deals with disqualification for Panchayat membership.

article_title: Powers, authority and responsibilities of Panchayats
summary: Allows States to give Panchayats powers for local self-government and planning.

article_title: Powers to impose taxes by, and funds of, the Panchayats
summary: Allows Panchayats to receive funds and levy taxes as authorised by State law.

article_title: Constitution of Finance Commission to review financial position
summary: Requires State Finance Commissions to review local body finances.

article_title: Audit of accounts of Panchayats
summary: Allows State law to provide for Panchayat accounts and audit.

article_title: Elections to the Panchayats
summary: Vests Panchayat election supervision in the State Election Commission.

article_title: Application to Union territories
summary: Applies Panchayat provisions to Union territories with modifications.

article_title: Part not to apply to certain areas
summary: Excludes certain tribal, scheduled and special areas from automatic application.

article_title: Continuance of existing laws and Panchayats
summary: Protected existing Panchayat laws temporarily after the 73rd Amendment.

article_title: Bar to interference by courts in electoral matters
summary: Bars courts from interfering in Panchayat elections except through election petitions.

article_title: Definitions
summary: Defines key terms used in the Municipalities Part.

article_title: Constitution of Municipalities
summary: Requires Nagar Panchayats, Municipal Councils and Municipal Corporations.

article_title: Composition of Municipalities
summary: Deals with municipal composition and representation.

article_title: Constitution and composition of Wards Committees, etc.
summary: Provides for Wards Committees in larger municipal areas.

article_title: Reservation of seats
summary: Provides reservation in municipalities for SCs, STs and women.

article_title: Duration of Municipalities, etc.
summary: Fixes municipal term at five years and provides election rules after dissolution.

article_title: Disqualifications for membership
summary: Deals with disqualification for municipal membership.

article_title: Powers, authority and responsibilities of Municipalities, etc.
summary: Allows States to give municipalities powers for urban self-government and planning.

article_title: Power to impose taxes by, and funds of, the Municipalities
summary: Allows municipalities to receive funds and levy taxes as authorised by State law.

article_title: Finance Commission
summary: Extends State Finance Commission review to municipal finances.

article_title: Audit of accounts of Municipalities
summary: Allows State law to provide for municipal accounts and audit.

article_title: Elections to the Municipalities
summary: Vests municipal election supervision in the State Election Commission.

article_title: Application to Union territories
summary: Applies municipal provisions to Union territories with modifications.

article_title: Part not to apply to certain areas
summary: Excludes certain tribal, scheduled and special areas from automatic municipal provisions.

article_title: Committee for district planning
summary: Provides for District Planning Committees to consolidate local plans.

article_title: Committee for Metropolitan planning
summary: Provides for Metropolitan Planning Committees in metropolitan areas.

article_title: Continuance of existing laws and Municipalities
summary: Protected existing municipal laws temporarily after the 74th Amendment.

article_title: Bar to interference by courts in electoral matters
summary: Bars courts from interfering in municipal elections except through election petitions.

article_title: Definitions
summary: Defines key terms used for co-operative societies.

article_title: Incorporation of co-operative societies
summary: Allows State law to regulate incorporation and democratic functioning of co-operatives.

article_title: Number and term of members of board and its office bearers
summary: Deals with board composition and tenure of co-operative societies.

article_title: Election of members of board
summary: Deals with timely elections to co-operative society boards.

article_title: Supersession and suspension of board and interim management
summary: Limits supersession or suspension of co-operative society boards.

article_title: Audit of accounts of co-operative societies
summary: Deals with audit of co-operative society accounts.

article_title: Convening of general body meetings
summary: Requires general body meetings of co-operative societies.

article_title: Right of a member to get information
summary: Gives members information rights in co-operative societies.

article_title: Returns
summary: Requires co-operative societies to file returns.

article_title: Offences and penalties
summary: Allows law to define offences and penalties for co-operative societies.

article_title: Application to multi-State co-operative societies
summary: Applies co-operative provisions to multi-State co-operative societies with modifications.

article_title: Application to Union territories
summary: Applies co-operative provisions to Union territories.

article_title: Continuance of existing laws
summary: Protected existing co-operative laws temporarily after the 97th Amendment.

article_title: Administration of Scheduled Areas and Tribal Areas
summary: Applies Fifth and Sixth Schedule systems for Scheduled Areas and Tribal Areas.

article_title: Formation of an autonomous State comprising certain tribal areas in Assam
summary: Allows creation of an autonomous State within Assam for certain tribal areas.

article_title: Extent of laws made by Parliament and by the Legislatures of States
part_title: Relations between the Union and the States
summary: Defines territorial extent of Union and State legislative powers.

article_title: Subject-matter of laws made by Parliament and by the Legislatures of States
part_title: Relations between the Union and the States
summary: Distributes law-making power through Union, State and Concurrent Lists.

article_title: Special provision with respect to goods and services tax
part_title: Relations between the Union and the States
summary: Gives Parliament and States power to make GST laws.

article_title: Power of Parliament to provide for the establishment of certain additional courts
part_title: Relations between the Union and the States
summary: Allows Parliament to create additional courts for Union laws.

article_title: Residuary powers of legislation
part_title: Relations between the Union and the States
summary: Gives Parliament law-making power on matters not in State or Concurrent Lists.

article_title: Power of Parliament to legislate with respect to a matter in the State List in the national interest
part_title: Relations between the Union and the States
summary: Allows Parliament to legislate on State List matters if Rajya Sabha declares national interest.

article_title: Power of Parliament to legislate with respect to any matter in the State List if a Proclamation of Emergency is in operation
part_title: Relations between the Union and the States
summary: Allows Parliament to legislate on State List matters during National Emergency.

article_title: Inconsistency between laws made by Parliament under articles 249 and 250 and laws made by the Legislatures of States
part_title: Relations between the Union and the States
summary: Gives priority to Parliament’s laws made under Articles 249 or 250 over inconsistent State laws.

article_title: Power of Parliament to legislate for two or more States by consent
part_title: Relations between the Union and the States
summary: Allows Parliament to legislate for consenting States on State List matters.

article_title: Legislation for giving effect to international agreements
part_title: Relations between the Union and the States
summary: Allows Parliament to make laws to implement treaties and international agreements.

article_title: Inconsistency between laws made by Parliament and laws made by the Legislatures of States
part_title: Relations between the Union and the States
summary: Deals with conflict between Union and State laws on Concurrent List matters.

article_title: Requirements as to recommendations and previous sanctions to be regarded as matters of procedure only
part_title: Relations between the Union and the States
summary: Treats certain recommendation or sanction requirements as procedural.

article_title: Obligation of States and the Union
part_title: Relations between the Union and the States
summary: Requires States to comply with Union laws and allows Union directions.

article_title: Control of the Union over States in certain cases
part_title: Relations between the Union and the States
summary: Allows Union directions to States on matters affecting Union executive power.

article_title: Assistance to States by deployment of armed forces or other forces of the Union
part_title: Relations between the Union and the States
summary: Omitted article that dealt with Union deployment of armed forces to assist States.

article_title: Power of the Union to confer powers, etc., on States in certain cases
part_title: Relations between the Union and the States
summary: Allows Union functions to be entrusted to States.

article_title: Power of the States to entrust functions to the Union
part_title: Relations between the Union and the States
summary: Allows States to entrust functions to the Union.

article_title: Repealed
part_title: Relations between the Union and the States
summary: Repealed provision related to armed forces in Part B States.

article_title: Jurisdiction of the Union in relation to territories outside India
part_title: Relations between the Union and the States
summary: Allows India to exercise functions in foreign territories by agreement.

article_title: Public acts, records and judicial proceedings
part_title: Relations between the Union and the States
summary: Requires full faith and credit to public acts, records and judicial proceedings.

article_title: Adjudication of disputes relating to waters of inter-State rivers or river valleys
part_title: Relations between the Union and the States
summary: Allows Parliament to provide adjudication of inter-State river water disputes.

article_title: Provisions with respect to an inter-State Council
part_title: Relations between the Union and the States
summary: Allows creation of Inter-State Council for coordination and dispute discussion.

article_title: Interpretation
summary: Defines terms used in the finance chapter.

article_title: Taxes not to be imposed save by authority of law
summary: Says no tax can be levied or collected without legal authority.

article_title: Consolidated Funds and public accounts of India and of the States
summary: Creates Consolidated Fund and Public Account for Union and States.

article_title: Contingency Fund
summary: Provides for Union and State Contingency Funds for urgent expenditure.

article_title: Duties levied by the Union but collected and appropriated by the States
summary: Deals with duties levied by Union but collected and used by States.

article_title: Service tax levied by Union and collected and appropriated by the Union and the States
summary: Omitted after GST replaced the earlier service tax sharing structure.

article_title: Taxes levied and collected by the Union but assigned to the States
summary: Deals with taxes collected by Union but assigned to States.

article_title: Levy and collection of goods and services tax in course of inter-State trade or commerce
summary: Deals with IGST on inter-State trade and import supplies.

article_title: Taxes levied and distributed between the Union and the States
summary: Deals with tax distribution between Union and States.

article_title: Surcharge on certain duties and taxes for purposes of the Union
summary: Allows Union surcharges that are not shared with States.

article_title: Repealed
summary: Repealed article on Union duties and State distribution.

article_title: Grants in lieu of export duty on jute and jute products
summary: Provided grants related to jute export duty to certain States.

article_title: Prior recommendation of President required to Bills affecting taxation in which States are interested
summary: Requires President’s recommendation for certain tax Bills affecting States.

article_title: Grants from the Union to certain States
summary: Allows statutory grants-in-aid to States, including tribal welfare grants.

article_title: Taxes on professions, trades, callings and employments
summary: Allows States to levy profession tax within constitutional limits.

article_title: Savings
summary: Saved certain pre-Constitution taxes and duties.

article_title: Repealed
summary: Repealed provision on agreements with Part B States.

article_title: Calculation of net proceeds, etc.
summary: Deals with calculation and certification of net tax proceeds.

article_title: Goods and Services Tax Council
summary: Creates GST Council and defines its composition and functions.

article_title: Finance Commission
summary: Creates Finance Commission to recommend tax sharing and grants.

article_title: Recommendations of the Finance Commission
summary: Requires Finance Commission recommendations to be laid before Parliament.

article_title: Expenditure defrayable by the Union or a State out of its revenues
summary: Allows Union or State grants for public purposes beyond normal legislative fields.

article_title: Custody, etc., of Consolidated Funds, Contingency Funds and public accounts
summary: Deals with custody and payment rules for public funds.

article_title: Custody of suitors’ deposits and other moneys received by public servants and courts
summary: Deals with custody of court deposits and public servant receipts.

article_title: Exemption of property of the Union from State taxation
summary: Exempts Union property from State taxation unless Parliament allows otherwise.

article_title: Restrictions as to imposition of tax on the sale or purchase of goods
summary: Restricts State taxation on certain inter-State, import and export transactions.

article_title: Exemption from taxes on electricity
summary: Gives certain exemptions from State taxes on electricity.

article_title: Exemption from taxation by States in respect of water or electricity in certain cases
summary: Restricts State taxes on water or electricity in specified cases.

article_title: Exemption of property and income of a State from Union taxation
summary: Exempts State property and income from Union taxation with exceptions.

article_title: Adjustment in respect of certain expenses and pensions
summary: Deals with financial adjustments for certain expenses and pensions.

article_title: Annual payment to certain Devaswom Funds
summary: Provides annual payments to specified Devaswom funds.

article_title: Privy purse sums of Rulers
summary: Omitted article that recognised privy purse payments to former rulers.

article_title: Borrowing by the Government of India
summary: Allows the Union Government to borrow on the security of the Consolidated Fund of India.

article_title: Borrowing by States
summary: Deals with State borrowing and Union consent when States owe Union loans.

article_title: Succession to property, assets, rights, liabilities and obligations in certain cases
summary: Transfers certain pre-Constitution assets and liabilities to Union and States.

article_title: Succession to property, assets, rights, liabilities and obligations in other cases
summary: Deals with succession of assets and liabilities in other constitutional transition cases.

article_title: Property accruing by escheat or lapse or as bona vacantia
summary: Assigns ownerless property to Union or States depending on location.

article_title: Things of value within territorial waters or continental shelf and resources of the exclusive economic zone to vest in the Union
summary: Vests marine resources and things of value in the Union.

article_title: Power to carry on trade, etc.
summary: Allows Union and States to carry on trade or business and acquire property.

article_title: Contracts
summary: Lays down how government contracts must be made.

article_title: Suits and proceedings
summary: Allows Union and States to sue and be sued.

article_title: Persons not to be deprived of property save by authority of law
summary: Makes property a constitutional legal right, not a Fundamental Right.

article_title: Freedom of trade, commerce and intercourse
part_title: Trade, Commerce and Intercourse within the Territory of India
summary: Guarantees free trade, commerce and intercourse throughout India.

article_title: Power of Parliament to impose restrictions on trade, commerce and intercourse
part_title: Trade, Commerce and Intercourse within the Territory of India
summary: Allows Parliament to impose public-interest restrictions on trade freedom.

article_title: Restrictions on legislative powers of the Union and of the States with regard to trade and commerce
part_title: Trade, Commerce and Intercourse within the Territory of India
summary: Restricts discriminatory preference between States in trade laws.

article_title: Restrictions on trade, commerce and intercourse among States
part_title: Trade, Commerce and Intercourse within the Territory of India
summary: Allows States to impose non-discriminatory taxes and reasonable restrictions on trade.

article_title: Saving of existing laws and laws providing for State monopolies
part_title: Trade, Commerce and Intercourse within the Territory of India
summary: Saves existing laws and laws creating State monopolies.

article_title: Repealed
part_title: Trade, Commerce and Intercourse within the Territory of India
summary: Repealed article related to trade restrictions by certain Part B States.

article_title: Appointment of authority for carrying out the purposes of articles 301 to 304
part_title: Trade, Commerce and Intercourse within the Territory of India
summary: Allows Parliament to appoint an authority for inter-State trade provisions.

article_title: Interpretation
part_title: Services under the Union and the States
summary: Defines terms used in the services Part.

article_title: Recruitment and conditions of service of persons serving the Union or a State
part_title: Services under the Union and the States
summary: Allows law or rules for recruitment and service conditions of public servants.

article_title: Tenure of office of persons serving the Union or a State
part_title: Services under the Union and the States
summary: Embodies the doctrine of pleasure for civil servants, subject to constitutional safeguards.

article_title: Dismissal, removal or reduction in rank of persons employed in civil capacities under the Union or a State
part_title: Services under the Union and the States
summary: Protects civil servants against dismissal, removal or reduction without due safeguards.

article_title: All-India services
part_title: Services under the Union and the States
summary: Allows creation of All-India Services if Rajya Sabha declares it necessary.

article_title: Power of Parliament to vary or revoke conditions of service of officers of certain services
part_title: Services under the Union and the States
summary: Allows Parliament to vary or revoke service conditions of certain pre-Constitution officers.

article_title: Transitional provisions
part_title: Services under the Union and the States
summary: Continues existing service rules until replaced.

article_title: Provision for protection of existing officers of certain services
part_title: Services under the Union and the States
summary: Omitted provision that protected certain pre-Constitution service officers.

article_title: Public Service Commissions for the Union and for the States
part_title: Services under the Union and the States
summary: Creates UPSC and State Public Service Commissions.

article_title: Appointment and term of office of members
part_title: Services under the Union and the States
summary: Deals with appointment and tenure of Public Service Commission members.

article_title: Removal and suspension of a member of a Public Service Commission
part_title: Services under the Union and the States
summary: Deals with removal and suspension of PSC members.

article_title: Power to make regulations as to conditions of service of members and staff of the Commission
part_title: Services under the Union and the States
summary: Allows service regulations for PSC members and staff.

article_title: Prohibition as to holding of offices by members of Commission on ceasing to be such members
part_title: Services under the Union and the States
summary: Restricts post-tenure appointments of PSC members.

article_title: Functions of Public Service Commissions
part_title: Services under the Union and the States
summary: Lists recruitment and advisory functions of Public Service Commissions.

article_title: Power to extend functions of Public Service Commissions
part_title: Services under the Union and the States
summary: Allows law to extend PSC functions.

article_title: Expenses of Public Service Commissions
part_title: Services under the Union and the States
summary: Charges PSC expenses on the relevant Consolidated Fund.

article_title: Reports of Public Service Commissions
part_title: Services under the Union and the States
summary: Requires PSC annual reports to be placed before the legislature.

article_title: Administrative tribunals
summary: Allows Parliament to create tribunals for public service disputes.

article_title: Tribunals for other matters
summary: Allows legislatures to create tribunals for specified subject matters.

article_title: Superintendence, direction and control of elections to be vested in an Election Commission
summary: Creates Election Commission and gives it control over elections.

article_title: No person to be ineligible for inclusion in electoral roll on grounds of religion, race, caste or sex
summary: Prohibits exclusion from electoral rolls on certain identity grounds.

article_title: Elections to the House of the People and to the Legislative Assemblies of States to be on the basis of adult suffrage
summary: Provides universal adult suffrage for Lok Sabha and State Assembly elections.

article_title: Power of Parliament to make provision with respect to elections to Legislatures
summary: Allows Parliament to make election laws.

article_title: Power of Legislature of a State to make provision with respect to elections to such Legislature
summary: Allows State Legislatures to make election laws where Parliament has not done so.

article_title: Bar to interference by courts in electoral matters
summary: Bars courts from interfering in elections except through election petitions.

article_title: Special provision as to elections to Parliament in the case of Prime Minister and Speaker
summary: Omitted article inserted during Emergency to shield certain elections from judicial review.

article_title: Reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in the House of the People
part_title: Special Provisions Relating to Certain Classes
summary: Provides Lok Sabha seat reservation for SCs and STs.

article_title: Reservation of seats for women in the House of the People
part_title: Special Provisions Relating to Certain Classes
summary: Provides women’s reservation in Lok Sabha after delimitation conditions are met.

article_title: Representation of the Anglo-Indian community in the House of the People
part_title: Special Provisions Relating to Certain Classes
summary: Earlier allowed Anglo-Indian nomination to Lok Sabha; this has ceased after the 104th Amendment period.

article_title: Reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in the Legislative Assemblies of the States
part_title: Special Provisions Relating to Certain Classes
summary: Provides State Assembly seat reservation for SCs and STs.

article_title: Reservation of seats for women in the Legislative Assemblies of the States
part_title: Special Provisions Relating to Certain Classes
summary: Provides women’s reservation in State Assemblies after delimitation conditions are met.

article_title: Representation of the Anglo-Indian community in the Legislative Assemblies of the States
part_title: Special Provisions Relating to Certain Classes
summary: Earlier allowed Anglo-Indian nomination to State Assemblies; this has ceased after the 104th Amendment period.

article_title: Reservation of seats and special representation to cease after certain period
part_title: Special Provisions Relating to Certain Classes
summary: Sets duration for SC/ST reservation and earlier Anglo-Indian representation.

article_title: Reservation of seats for women to take effect
part_title: Special Provisions Relating to Certain Classes
summary: Specifies when women’s reservation will begin and how long it will last.

article_title: Claims of Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes to services and posts
part_title: Special Provisions Relating to Certain Classes
summary: Balances SC/ST service claims with administrative efficiency.

article_title: Special provision for Anglo-Indian community in certain services
part_title: Special Provisions Relating to Certain Classes
summary: Provided transitional service protections for Anglo-Indians.

article_title: Special provision with respect to educational grants for the benefit of Anglo-Indian community
part_title: Special Provisions Relating to Certain Classes
summary: Provided transitional educational grants for Anglo-Indians.

article_title: National Commission for Scheduled Castes
part_title: Special Provisions Relating to Certain Classes
summary: Creates National Commission for Scheduled Castes and defines its functions.

article_title: National Commission for Scheduled Tribes
part_title: Special Provisions Relating to Certain Classes
summary: Creates National Commission for Scheduled Tribes and defines its functions.

article_title: National Commission for Backward Classes
part_title: Special Provisions Relating to Certain Classes
summary: Creates National Commission for Backward Classes as a constitutional body.

article_title: Control of the Union over the administration of Scheduled Areas and the welfare of Scheduled Tribes
part_title: Special Provisions Relating to Certain Classes
summary: Allows Union oversight for Scheduled Areas and ST welfare.

article_title: Appointment of a Commission to investigate the conditions of backward classes
part_title: Special Provisions Relating to Certain Classes
summary: Allows appointment of commissions to study backward classes and recommend measures.

article_title: Scheduled Castes
part_title: Special Provisions Relating to Certain Classes
summary: Deals with presidential notification and parliamentary modification of Scheduled Castes lists.

article_title: Scheduled Tribes
part_title: Special Provisions Relating to Certain Classes
summary: Deals with presidential notification and parliamentary modification of Scheduled Tribes lists.

article_title: Socially and educationally backward classes
part_title: Special Provisions Relating to Certain Classes
summary: Deals with identification of socially and educationally backward classes.

article_title: Official language of the Union
summary: Declares Hindi in Devanagari script as Union official language with English continuation rules.

article_title: Commission and Committee of Parliament on official language
summary: Provides commission and parliamentary committee for official language issues.

article_title: Official language or languages of a State
summary: Allows States to adopt official language or languages.

article_title: Official language for communication between one State and another or between a State and the Union
summary: Deals with official language for inter-State and Union-State communication.

article_title: Special provision relating to language spoken by a section of the population of a State
summary: Allows recognition of a language spoken by a section of a State’s population.

article_title: Language to be used in the Supreme Court and High Courts and for Acts, Bills, etc.
summary: Provides for English in higher courts and authoritative legal texts unless otherwise allowed.

article_title: Special procedure for enactment of certain laws relating to language
summary: Sets special procedure for some language-related laws.

article_title: Language to be used in representations for redress of grievances
summary: Allows people to submit grievances in any language used in the Union or State.

article_title: Facilities for instruction in mother-tongue at primary stage
summary: Directs States to provide mother-tongue primary education for linguistic minorities.

article_title: Special Officer for linguistic minorities
summary: Creates Special Officer for linguistic minorities.

article_title: Directive for development of the Hindi language
summary: Directs the Union to promote and develop Hindi.

article_title: Proclamation of Emergency
summary: Deals with National Emergency due to war, external aggression or armed rebellion.

article_title: Effect of Proclamation of Emergency
summary: Explains Union power expansion during National Emergency.

article_title: Application of provisions relating to distribution of revenues while a Proclamation of Emergency is in operation
summary: Allows revenue distribution modifications during Emergency.

article_title: Duty of the Union to protect States against external aggression and internal disturbance
summary: Makes Union responsible for protecting States and ensuring constitutional governance.

article_title: Provisions in case of failure of constitutional machinery in States
summary: Provides for President’s Rule in States.

article_title: Exercise of legislative powers under Proclamation issued under article 356
summary: Explains legislative power during President’s Rule.

article_title: Suspension of provisions of article 19 during emergencies
summary: Suspends Article 19 during certain National Emergencies.

article_title: Suspension of the enforcement of the rights conferred by Part III during emergencies
summary: Allows suspension of court enforcement of specified Fundamental Rights during Emergency.

article_title: Application of this Part to the State of Punjab
summary: Omitted special emergency-related provision for Punjab.

article_title: Provisions as to financial emergency
summary: Provides for Financial Emergency if India’s financial stability is threatened.

article_title: Protection of President and Governors and Rajpramukhs
summary: Gives immunity to President and Governors for official acts and limits legal proceedings against them.

article_title: Protection of publication of proceedings of Parliament and State Legislatures
summary: Protects fair publication of legislative proceedings.

article_title: Disqualification for appointment on remunerative political post
summary: Bars certain defectors from remunerative political posts.

article_title: Rights and privileges of Rulers of Indian States
summary: Omitted article that recognised rights and privileges of former rulers.

article_title: Bar to interference by courts in disputes arising out of certain treaties, agreements, etc.
summary: Bars courts from disputes arising from certain pre-Constitution treaties and agreements.

article_title: Recognition granted to Rulers of Indian States to cease and privy purses to be abolished
summary: Abolished recognition of former rulers and privy purses.

article_title: Special provisions as to major ports and aerodromes
summary: Allows special legal treatment for major ports and aerodromes.

article_title: Effect of failure to comply with, or to give effect to, directions given by the Union
summary: Allows non-compliance with Union directions to support action under Article 356.

article_title: Definitions
summary: Defines important constitutional terms.

article_title: Interpretation
summary: Provides interpretation rules for the Constitution.

article_title: Power of Parliament to amend the Constitution and procedure therefor
summary: Lays down Parliament’s power and procedure to amend the Constitution.

article_title: Temporary power to Parliament to make laws with respect to certain matters in the State List as if they were matters in the Concurrent List
summary: Gave temporary Union power over certain State List matters.

article_title: Temporary provisions with respect to the State of Jammu and Kashmir
summary: Gave special constitutional status to Jammu and Kashmir before the 2019 constitutional changes.

article_title: Special provision with respect to the States of Maharashtra and Gujarat
summary: Allows special development boards and arrangements for Maharashtra and Gujarat.

article_title: Special provision with respect to the State of Nagaland
summary: Protects Naga religious, social practices, customary law and land resources.

article_title: Special provision with respect to the State of Assam
summary: Allows special legislative committee arrangements for tribal areas of Assam.

article_title: Special provision with respect to the State of Manipur
summary: Allows special committee arrangements for hill areas of Manipur.

article_title: Special provisions with respect to the State of Andhra Pradesh or the State of Telangana
summary: Provides special arrangements for equitable opportunities in Andhra Pradesh and Telangana.

article_title: Establishment of Central University in Andhra Pradesh
summary: Allows establishment of a Central University in Andhra Pradesh.

article_title: Special provisions with respect to the State of Sikkim
summary: Provides special constitutional arrangements for Sikkim after its merger with India.

article_title: Special provision with respect to the State of Mizoram
summary: Protects Mizo religious, social practices, customary law and land ownership.

article_title: Special provision with respect to the State of Arunachal Pradesh
summary: Gives the Governor special responsibility for law and order in Arunachal Pradesh.

article_title: Special provision with respect to the State of Goa
summary: Provides minimum Assembly size for Goa.

article_title: Special provisions with respect to the State of Karnataka
summary: Provides special development and reservation arrangements for Hyderabad-Karnataka region.

article_title: Continuance in force of existing laws and their adaptation
summary: Continues pre-Constitution laws until changed or repealed.

article_title: Power of the President to adapt laws
summary: Gave President power to adapt laws during constitutional transition.

article_title: Power of President to make order in respect of persons under preventive detention in certain cases
summary: Gave temporary preventive detention-related powers to the President.

article_title: Provisions as to Judges of the Federal Court and proceedings pending in the Federal Court or before His Majesty in Council
summary: Transferred Federal Court judges and pending cases into the new Supreme Court system.

article_title: Courts, authorities and officers to continue to function subject to the provisions of the Constitution
summary: Continued existing courts, authorities and officers after commencement of Constitution.

article_title: Provisions as to Judges of High Courts
summary: Continued existing High Court judges under the new Constitution.

article_title: Provisions as to Comptroller and Auditor-General of India
summary: Continued the existing Auditor-General as CAG under the Constitution.

article_title: Provisions as to Public Service Commissions
summary: Continued existing Public Service Commissions after commencement of Constitution.

article_title: Special provision as to duration of Andhra Pradesh Legislative Assembly
summary: Omitted special transitional provision for Andhra Pradesh Assembly duration.

article_title: Repealed
summary: Repealed transitional provision relating to provisional Parliament.

article_title: Repealed
summary: Repealed transitional provision related to the President.

article_title: Repealed
summary: Repealed transitional provision related to Council of States.

article_title: Repealed
summary: Repealed transitional provision related to State Legislatures.

article_title: Repealed
summary: Repealed transitional provision.

article_title: Repealed
summary: Repealed transitional provision.

article_title: Repealed
summary: Repealed transitional provision.

article_title: Repealed
summary: Repealed transitional provision.

article_title: Repealed
summary: Repealed transitional provision.

article_title: Repealed
summary: Repealed transitional provision.

article_title: Repealed
summary: Repealed transitional provision.

article_title: Repealed
summary: Repealed transitional provision.

article_title: Repealed
summary: Repealed transitional provision.

article_title: Power of the President to remove difficulties
summary: Gave the President temporary power to remove constitutional difficulties.

article_title: Short title
part_title: Short Title, Commencement, Authoritative Text in Hindi and Repeals
summary: Names the Constitution as the Constitution of India.

article_title: Commencement
part_title: Short Title, Commencement, Authoritative Text in Hindi and Repeals
summary: States when different constitutional provisions came into force.

article_title: Authoritative text in the Hindi language
part_title: Short Title, Commencement, Authoritative Text in Hindi and Repeals
summary: Provides for authoritative Hindi text of the Constitution.

article_title: Repeals
part_title: Short Title, Commencement, Authoritative Text in Hindi and Repeals
summary: Repeals the Indian Independence Act, 1947 and Government of India Act, 1935.
